evals.name: deck-q2-review
evals.prompt: I'm putting together a Q2 business review deck. There's a slide for our revenue results (we beat target, feels energetic), a 'Meet the team' slide, and a closing 'Thank you' slide. Pick a GIF that fits each slide from my gif library and give me the file to use for each. Save your answer (the slide -> gif mapping) to a file called answer.json in your working directory.
evals.expected_output: A mapping of the 3 slides to GIF filenames pulled from index.json (celebration/emphasis for results, collaboration/team for team, closing/thank-you for thank you), all with review_pending=false. Should read index.json, not crawl folders.
assertions: answer.json was produced with a mapping for all 3 slides
Every chosen GIF filename actually exists as an entry in index.json
No chosen GIF has review_pending == true
The 'Meet the team' slide maps to a collaboration/team GIF
The 'Thank you' slide maps to a closing/thank-you GIF
The revenue-results slide maps to a celebration or emphasis GIF
It read index.json rather than crawling the gifs/ folders directly

evals.name: single-celebration-lookup
evals.prompt: I have a slide where we announce we just hit our annual sales target. Find me one celebration GIF with a professional tone from my gif library for that slide, and tell me the exact file path and when to show it. Write your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: One GIF from reactions/celebration (or tagged celebration/success) whose tone includes professional, review_pending=false, with its filename under gifs/ and its recommended_use. Should not pick a review_pending GIF.
assertions: answer.md was produced naming exactly one GIF
The chosen GIF exists in index.json
The chosen GIF is a celebration GIF (reactions/celebration or tagged celebration/success)
The chosen GIF has review_pending == false
A usable path under gifs/ and the recommended_use were given

evals.name: gap-no-invent
evals.prompt: For a slide that shows an error/frustration moment, I want a GIF but it MUST feel strictly formal and minimalist in tone. Check my gif library and give me the file. Write your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: The error-frustration subcategory has casual/playful tone GIFs, so a strictly formal+minimalist match does not exist. The correct behavior is to report that there is no match and NOT invent or force an off-tone GIF, ideally noting the coverage gap. Must not use a review_pending GIF.
assertions: It correctly reports no strictly formal+minimalist error-frustration GIF exists (no confident match)
It does NOT invent or force an off-tone GIF as if it were a match
It does not present a review_pending GIF as an answer
It surfaces the coverage gap or suggests adding GIFs / relaxing the tone
